abstract: Single-pixel imaging offers a powerful framework for capturing scenes without a direct line of sight to the object; however, maintaining high image fidelity remains a significant challenge, particularly in the presence of ambient noise. In this work, we present a single-pixel imaging method that achieves high-quality reconstruction by directly acquiring the scene's Fourier spectrum. By employing phase-shifted sinusoidal structured illumination, we can retrieve the complex spectrum and reconstruct the target image via an inverse Fourier transform. This approach removes the requirement for a direct view of the scene, allowing for flexible detector placement limited only by the ability to collect reflected light. Furthermore, the technique leverages a compressive sampling framework, enabling accurate image reconstruction from sub-Nyquist measurements. We experimentally demonstrate the robustness of this method by recovering clear images using a non-line-of-sight detector, even under conditions of substantial environmental illumination noise.
